ancestry_feature_1_description: Decide who made you and for what purpose. At character creation, choose one of your Experiences that best aligns with this purpose and gain a permanent +1 bonus to it'
ancestry_feature_2_description: When you take a short rest, you can choose a long rest move instead of a short rest move'

ancestry_feature_1_description: Your scales act as a natural protection. When you would take Severe damage, you can mark a Stress to mark 1 fewer Hit Points'
ancestry_feature_2_description: Choose an element for your breath (such as electricity, fire, or ice). You can use this breath against a target or a group of targets within very Close Range, treating it as an Instinct weapon that deals d8 magic damage using your proficiency'

ancestry_feature_1_description: When you take minor damage, you can mark 2 stress instead of marking a Hit Point'
ancestry_feature_2_description: Spend 3 hope to halve incoming physical damage'

ancestry_feature_1_description: Mark a Stress to gain advantage on a reaction roll'
ancestry_feature_2_description: During a rest, you can drop into a trance to choose an additional downtime move'

ancestry_feature_1_description: Once per session, after you or a willing ally within close range makes an action roll, you can spend 3 hope to reroll the duality dice'
You can leap anywhere within Close range as though you were using normal movement, allowing you to vault obstacles, jump across gaps, or scale barriers with ease'
ancestry_feature_2_description: When you succeed on an attack against a target within melee range, you can mark a stress to kick yourself off them, dealing an extra 2d6 damage and knocking back either yourself or the target to Very Close range '

ancestry_feature_1_description: When you succeed on an Agility Roll to move from Far or Very Far range into Melee range with one or more targets, you can mark a Stress to deal 1d12 physical damage to all targets within Melee range'
ancestry_feature_2_description: When you would mark a Stress, roll a d6. On a result of 6, do not mark it'

ancestry_feature_1_description: Make an Instinct Roll (12) to use your mycelial array to speak with others of your ancestry. On a success, you can communicate across any distance'
ancestry_feature_2_description: While touching a corpse that died recently, you can mark a Stress to extract one memory from the corpse related to a specific emotion or sensation of your choice'

ancestry_feature_1_description: Gain a bonus to your damage thresholds equal to your Proficiency'
ancestry_feature_2_description: Mark a Stress to retract into your shell. While in your shell, you have resistance to physical damage, you have disadvantage on action rolls, and you cannot move'

ancestry_feature_1_description: Gain an additional Hit Point slot at character creation'
ancestry_feature_2_description: Treat any weapon, ability, spell, or other feature that has a Melee range as though it has a Very Close range instead'

ancestry_feature_1_description: You ignore disadvantage on Agility Rolls'
ancestry_feature_2_description: Once per rest, mark a Stress to force an adversary to reroll an attack against you or an ally within Very Close range'

ancestry_feature_1_description: At the start of each session, everyone in your party gains a Hope'
ancestry_feature_2_description: When you roll a 1 on your Hope Die, you can reroll it'

ancestry_feature_1_description: Gain an additional Stress slot at character creation'
ancestry_feature_2_description: When you fail a roll that utilized one of your Experiences, you can mark a Stress to reroll'

ancestry_feature_1_description: When you roll with Fear, you can mark 2 Stress to change it into a roll with Hope instead'
ancestry_feature_2_description: You have advantage on rolls to intimidate hostile creatures'

ancestry_feature_1_description: When you make an Agility Roll, you can spend 2 Hope to reroll your Hope Die'
ancestry_feature_2_description: Make an Agility Roll to scratch a target within Melee range. On a success, they become temporarily Vulnerable'

ancestry_feature_1_description: When you have 1 Hit Point remaining, attacks against you have disadvantage'
ancestry_feature_2_description: When you succeed on an attack against a target within Melee range, you can spend a Hope to gore the target with your tusks, dealing an extra 1d6 damage'

ancestry_feature_1_description: You can breathe and move naturally underwater'
ancestry_feature_2_description: You can use your long tongue to grab onto things within Close range. Mark a Stress to use your tongue as a Finesse Close weapon that deals d12 physical damage using your Proficiency'

ancestry_feature_1_description: You have advantage on Agility Rolls that involve balancing and climbing'
ancestry_feature_2_description: Gain a permanent +1 bonus to your Evasion at character creation'
